# Spec: JeekLee's playground — Design System v1

**Date:** 2026-05-16
**Status:** Accepted
**Supersedes:** the ad-hoc M1 token table in `docs/design/M1-identity.md` (which used `#3D63DD` accent and Inter-only — those decisions are replaced wholesale by this document).
**Applies to:** every user-facing surface of `client/` from M1 onward, plus the design generated by the `product-designer` agent in Stage 2 of every milestone.

## 1. Purpose

Pin a single, low-decision-cost design system so:
1. The `product-designer` agent does not re-invent the palette each milestone.
2. The `frontend-implementer` agent scaffolds `client/src/shared/ui/tokens/` once and never edits it without a superseding spec.
3. The site reads as **one place** across personal-tool surfaces (write, /me) and public-facing surfaces (blog, RAG chat, metrics).

This spec **does not** describe per-page UX (that lives in `docs/design/<Mx>-<slug>.md`). It describes the brand and the primitive vocabulary.

## 2. Brand

### 2.1 Identity (one line)

> *"매일 쓰는 개인 작업장이지만, 누가 들어와도 보여주고 싶은 곳."*
> A personal workshop that is also legible — and worth showing — to anyone who wanders in.

### 2.2 Wordmark

- **Full:** `JeekLee's playground`
- **In sidebar (stacked):** logo glyph `J` (26×26, accent fill, white text, radius 7px) + two-line text:
  - line 1: `JeekLee's` (13.5px / 700 / -0.01em)
  - line 2: `playground` (10px / 500 / +0.04em / uppercase, color `text.muted`)
- **In tight contexts (favicon, OG image):** glyph `J` only.

### 2.3 Tonal anchors

- **Notion-like warmth** — cream backgrounds, rounded surfaces, generous spacing.
- **Linear-like restraint** — single accent color, no decorative gradients in chrome, weight-driven hierarchy.
- **Editorial confidence** — body type at 15px (slightly larger than SaaS default) so prose pages don't feel "an app trying to read."
- **No serif headings.** B's typography decision (Inter throughout, weight-driven hierarchy) is final; do not add a display serif.

### 2.4 Public-vs-personal posture

The site is dual-mode by design. Logged-out visitors are **first-class readers**, not blockers to dismiss. The visual difference between the two modes is intentionally small:

| Surface | Public reader sees | Signed-in user sees |
|---|---|---|
| Header right | `Viewing publicly` neutral chip + `Sign in with Google` primary button | Account pill (avatar + name + chevron) |
| Sidebar footer | `Not signed in — Sign in to write/chat privately` | Avatar + email + Sign out |
| Sidebar Workspace section | Items present but with 🔒 lock badge | Items unlocked and active |
| Color / layout | identical | identical |

No two-theme split. No "marketing" vs "app" CSS. Same tokens everywhere.

## 3. Color tokens

Single source of truth. CSS variables on `:root` of the FSD `app` layer; downstream layers MUST NOT hardcode hex.

### 3.1 Surfaces / borders / text

| Token | Hex | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `color.bg` | `#FAF7EF` | App background |
| `color.surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Cards, inputs, modals |
| `color.surface.soft` | `#F4EFDF` | Sidebar background, disabled fills, kbd chips |
| `color.border` | `#E6E0CB` | Card strokes, dividers (default) |
| `color.border.strong` | `#D6CFB3` | Input borders, button outlines (need higher contrast) |
| `color.khaki` | `#C2B88A` | Avatar fallback, decorative underline, thumb gradients |
| `color.text` | `#2A2C20` | Body and headings |
| `color.text.muted` | `#6F6A55` | Subtitles, meta, secondary nav |
| `color.text.subtle` | `#8B8670` | Placeholders, footnotes, disabled labels |

### 3.2 Accent (single olive)

| Token | Hex | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `color.accent` | `#6E7A3A` | Primary CTA fill, active nav, focus ring, accent links |
| `color.accent.hover` | `#5C6730` | Primary CTA hover, link hover |
| `color.accent.soft` | `#E9E8D1` | Active nav background, accent chip background, ghost-button hover |

**Rule:** all primary affirmative interaction is `accent`. No second accent color. Khaki is decorative (not actionable).

### 3.3 Semantic

| Token | Hex | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `color.success` | `#4F6B2E` | "healthy" chips, success toast |
| `color.warning` | `#B58A2B` | "degraded" chips, warning toast |
| `color.danger` | `#B14B3B` | Destructive button fill, error text |
| `color.danger.soft` | `#F4E1DA` | Error background, danger chip background |
| `color.danger.hover` | `#9A3F31` | `danger` button hover fill |
| `color.info` | `#4A6B7A` | Info chips/toasts (rare) |
| `color.info.soft` | `#DCE4E9` | Info chip background |

Semantic colors are warm-shifted to live alongside the cream/olive palette without clashing. Do not substitute pure-red or pure-blue Tailwind defaults.

### 3.4 Dark mode

**Not in scope for M1–M5.** Token names are designed to swap cleanly — when dark mode lands, replicate the same names under `[data-theme="dark"]`. No component reads from a literal hex; this is the single invariant that makes a future swap a token-table change.

## 4. Typography

### 4.1 Family

- **UI + body:** `Inter` (variable). System fallback: `system-ui, -apple-system, "Segoe UI", sans-serif`.
- **Mono (code, numeric metrics, kbd):** `JetBrains Mono`. Fallback: `ui-monospace, "SF Mono", Menlo, monospace`.
- **No serif.** Decision made during brainstorming; reopen only with a superseding spec.

### 4.2 Scale (7 + 1)

| Token | Size | Weight | Tracking | Use |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `font.display` | 40px / 1.1 | 700 | -0.025em | Landing-style hero (rare — public marketing pages only) |
| `font.h1` | 28px / 1.2 | 700 | -0.02em | Page titles (`Sign in to continue`) |
| `font.h2` | 20px / 1.3 | 600 | -0.01em | Section titles (`Today's documents`) |
| `font.h3` | 16px / 1.4 | 600 | 0 | Card titles, sub-section |
| `font.body` | 15px / 1.6 | 400 | 0 | Default body, essay content |
| `font.small` | 13px / 1.5 | 400 | 0 | Meta, captions, helper |
| `font.eyebrow` | 11px / 1.2 | 600 | +0.14em / uppercase | Section labels, brand line over hero |
| `font.mono` | 13px | 400 | 0 | Code, kbd, numeric values |

Body at 15px is intentional and slightly larger than common SaaS defaults so blog/essay surfaces (M2 onward) read as prose, not as app chrome.

## 5. Spacing, radius, elevation

### 5.1 Spacing scale (T-shirt, multiples of 4)

`xs 4 / sm 8 / md 16 / lg 24 / xl 40 / 2xl 64`

### 5.2 Radius

`sm 6px (inputs, kbd) / md 10px (buttons, cards, tiles) / lg 14px (modals, page sections) / pill 999px (chips, avatar)`

### 5.3 Elevation

| Token | Value | Use |
|---|---|---|
| `shadow.card` | `0 4px 14px rgba(60,50,20,.05)` | Resting cards, tiles |
| `shadow.pop` | `0 10px 30px rgba(60,50,20,.10)` | Tile hover, modal, popover |

No third elevation level. If a third feels necessary, the layout is wrong.

### 5.4 Density

Medium. Sits between Linear (dense) and Vercel (airy). Default touch target 36px, default vertical rhythm 16px between siblings.

## 6. Component primitives (M1 set)

The four primitives below are the minimum frontend-implementer must ship in `client/src/shared/ui/` before any feature component lands. All read from tokens — zero literals.

### 6.1 Button

Five variants. All `13px / 500`, padding `8px 14px`, radius `md`, transition `all .14s ease`.

| Variant | bg | fg | border | Hover |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `primary` | `accent` | `#fff` | `accent` | `accent.hover` |
| `secondary` | `surface` | `text` | `border.strong` | bg → `surface.soft` |
| `ghost` | transparent | `accent` | transparent | bg → `accent.soft` |
| `danger` | `danger` | `#fff` | `danger` | `danger.hover` |
| `disabled` | `surface.soft` | `text.subtle` | `border` | (no change, `cursor: not-allowed`) |

Focus state on all: `outline: 2px solid accent; outline-offset: 1px`.

### 6.2 Input

Single variant. `14px`, padding `9px 12px`, radius `sm`, border `1px solid border.strong`, bg `surface`. Focus: `outline: 2px solid accent; outline-offset: 1px`.

Disabled state: bg `surface.soft`, color `text.subtle`, `cursor: not-allowed`.

### 6.3 Chip

`11px / 500`, padding `3px 9px`, radius `pill`. Six variants:

| Variant | bg | fg | Use |
|---|---|---|---|
| `accent` | `accent.soft` | `accent` | tag, badge |
| `neutral` | `surface.soft` | `text.muted` | `Viewing publicly`, low-weight meta |
| `success` | `#E5EBD9` | `success` | health "● healthy" |
| `warning` | `#F4E8C7` | `warning` | "● degraded" |
| `danger` | `danger.soft` | `danger` | "● down", error meta |
| `info` | `#DCE4E9` | `info` | rare; informational toast meta |

### 6.4 Card

bg `surface`, border `1px solid border`, radius `md`, padding `16px`, shadow `shadow.card`. Title `font.h3`, body `font.small` in `text.muted`.

Hover-as-link variant (used for blog post thumbnails and feature tiles): border → `accent`, transform `translateY(-2px)`, shadow → `shadow.pop`.

## 7. Iconography

- **Library:** Lucide (`lucide-react`), 1.5px stroke, 18px default, 24px in headers.
- **Color:** inherits `currentColor` from text. Decorative use only — every actionable icon must pair with a label or `aria-label`.
- **Emoji** is used in current mockups as placeholders for tile/section iconography. Frontend-implementer replaces with Lucide equivalents during M2 (e.g., 💬 → `MessageSquare`, 📖 → `BookOpen`, 📊 → `Activity`, ✍️ → `PenLine`).

## 8. Layout shell

The app uses a fixed two-column shell on `≥1024px` viewports.

### 8.1 Sidebar (232px, left)

> **Superseded by `2026-05-16-m2-docs-bc-design.md` §7.1** for the Apps section content and the locked-row treatment. The Brand row, Search palette trigger, Spacer, and Account footer described below remain in force. The Workspace section originally at item 4 is removed entirely.

Fixed width. Background `surface.soft`. Vertical sections, top-to-bottom:

1. **Brand row** — glyph + stacked wordmark.
2. **Search** — `⌘K` palette trigger. Surface-on-cream pill, `13px text.subtle`.
3. **Apps section** — single navigation section listing every top-level destination from M1, with locked previews for milestones not yet shipped. See M2 spec §7.1 for the canonical row list and locked-item / shipped-item treatment. Same preview-with-locks paradigm as the home tile grid (§9 below).
4. **Spacer** (flex: 1).
5. **Account footer** — avatar + name/email when signed in; `Not signed in — Sign in to write/chat privately` when not.

**Rule (Apps row visibility):** every top-level destination is listed from M1 onward. Rows whose milestone hasn't shipped render with the locked-item treatment (see M2 spec §7.1). The shipped vs. locked distinction is driven by GitHub milestone state (milestone closed = shipped). This replaces the earlier "strict grow rule" — empty Apps lists look uninhabited, full Apps lists with explicit milestone badges read as "here's what's coming."

Active state: `accent.soft` bg, `accent` text, weight 600. Hover (non-active): `surface` bg.

Collapsed mode (≥768px, <1024px): collapses to 64px icon-only rail. Below 768px: sidebar becomes a hamburger drawer over the main content.

### 8.2 Topbar (slim, in main column)

`padding: 12px 26px`, `border-bottom: 1px solid border`.

- Left: breadcrumb (current page name; `Home / Essays / Building an agent team` etc.)
- Right: status chip (`Viewing publicly` or signed-in account chip) + global action (`Sign in with Google` or quick-action button)

No second navigation bar. The sidebar owns navigation.

### 8.3 Main content area

Padding `26px 28px 32px`. Sections inside use 22–32px vertical rhythm.

## 9. Home page composition (the public landing)

> **Partially superseded by `2026-05-16-m2-docs-bc-design.md` §7.3.** From M2 onward the "Latest from the blog" section is renamed **"Latest from JeekLee's blog"** and sources only owner-authored public documents (the public-feed endpoint is owner-filtered). Layout and visual treatment described below are unchanged.

Order, top-to-bottom (inside main content area):

1. **Compact hero** (no big display type on home — display type lives on dedicated essays/marketing pages):
   - Eyebrow: `A PERSONAL PLATFORM · OPEN TO READ` (eyebrow style, accent color)
   - Title: `What would you like to do today?` (`font.h1`)
   - Subtitle: one line in `text.muted` describing the dual-mode posture.
2. **`Things you can try`** — section label + `See all →` link.
   - 4-column tile grid (one per shipped user-facing surface).
   - Tile = 36×36 colored icon, title, 1–2 line description, meta row (`PUBLIC` badge / count / status chip).
   - Locked tiles (sign-in required) render at 0.72 opacity with `🔒 sign in required` meta.
3. **`Latest from the blog`** — section label + `All essays →` link.
   - 3-column thumbnail grid (M2+).
   - Card = 124px gradient thumbnail (khaki / cream / sage variants), title (`font.h3`), 2-line excerpt (`font.small` muted), meta row with tag chip and `· N min · {date}`.
   - Until M2 ships, this section renders an empty-state card: `Essays will appear here when the blog is online. Track progress on GitHub.` linking to the M2 milestone.

At M1 release the home shows only the hero + a single tile (Home is the only shipped surface). Empty-state copy explicitly names which milestone unlocks each future tile, with a link to the GitHub milestone.

## 10. Implications for M1 (Identity) — already-produced design

The current `docs/design/M1-identity.md` is **partially superseded** by this system:

| Element | M1 doc currently says | Replace with |
|---|---|---|
| `color.accent` | `#3D63DD` | `#6E7A3A` (this spec §3.2) |
| Surface palette | white + slate | cream + warm border (this spec §3.1) |
| Typography | Inter, sizes `32/24/16/14/12` | Inter, scale from this spec §4.2 |
| Wordmark | `playground` | `JeekLee's playground` (this spec §2.2) |
| Home screen | Auth-only landing with `/me` JSON card | Public landing per this spec §9 (the `/me` debug view moves into the account footer or a `/me` route, not the home) |
| 401 screen | Standalone error route with full hero | Keep — but apply the new palette and use the secondary button for `Go home` |

**Action item (not in this spec, scheduled for next step):** re-dispatch `product-designer` for M1 with this spec as input. That work belongs to a writing-plans output, not to this design spec.

## 11. Architectural ripple — public routes

This spec assumes the platform serves logged-out visitors. ADR-07 (`07-gateway-oauth.md`) currently treats the gateway as OAuth-only ingress and assumes every backend trusts injected `X-User-Id`/`X-User-Email` headers. With public surfaces (M2 Essays read, M4 public Chat, M5 Metrics dashboard), the following are now required and must be resolved before the corresponding milestone's Stage 3:

1. **Public route allowlist at the gateway.** OAuth gate applies only to `Workspace`-scoped routes (`/api/docs/mine`, `/api/chat/private`, `/api/documents/*`, `/api/me`).
2. **Anonymous `X-User-Id` policy.** Public routes either receive no `X-User-Id` header, or a sentinel `anonymous` value. The downstream BC contract must be updated either way.
3. **Cost protection on public RAG chat.** Anonymous chat must be rate-limited and token-capped per IP/session, since it dispatches against `spark-inference-gateway` (Qwen3-32B) which is real compute. Concrete numbers belong in M4's per-milestone ADR; this spec only flags that the protection is required.
4. **Public-content scoping in retrieval.** Public chat retrieves only against documents the author marked public; private documents never enter the public retrieval scope. Document-visibility flag is an M2 schema concern.

**These four items must be reflected in PRD/ADR updates before M2 ships.** They are out of scope for this design system spec, but in scope for the next writing-plans cycle.

## 12. Deliverables (when this spec lands in code)

These belong to the next plan, not to this spec. Listed here so they aren't dropped:

- `client/src/shared/ui/tokens/` — CSS variables matching §3, §4, §5.
- `client/src/shared/ui/{Button,Input,Chip,Card}.tsx` — primitives from §6.
- `client/src/widgets/{Sidebar,Topbar}/` — layout shell from §8.
- `client/src/pages/home/` — landing composition from §9.
- ADR updates: `07-gateway-oauth.md` adds the public-route allowlist (§11).
- PRD updates: `M1-identity.md` notes the home-screen change; future PRDs (M2/M4/M5) carry their respective public-scope concerns from §11.
- Re-dispatch of `product-designer` for M1 to regenerate Figma frames against this spec (§10).

## 13. Out of scope (this spec)

- Per-page UX flows beyond the home (those are per-milestone design docs).
- Dark mode color values (deferred — token names are reserved).
- Animation / motion system (a separate spec when interactions become numerous; until then, defaults from §6 are sufficient).
- Mobile breakpoint specifics below 768px (a separate spec when the first mobile-priority surface appears, likely M4).
- Brand guidelines for off-product use (OG image generation, favicon refresh) — handled JIT.

## 14. Reopening this spec

Replace, do not edit:
- Change to accent color → new spec that supersedes §3.2.
- Add a serif → new spec that supersedes §4.1.
- Drop the sidebar → new spec that supersedes §8.
- Cosmetic tweaks (e.g., `border` lightened by 2 shades) can edit this doc directly with a `Changelog` entry at the bottom.

### Changelog
- 2026-05-16 — v1. Initial spec. Replaces ad-hoc M1 tokens.
